---
title: "Emerytura"
author: "Wiktoria Skolimowska,"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  dev = "cairo_pdf",
  echo = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  comment = FALSE,
  
  #fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 6,
  fig.align = "center"
)

options(digits = 3)

set.seed(17)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(reshape2)
library(gridExtra)
library(binom)
library(ggtext)
library(latex2exp)
library(DescTools)
library(car)
library(ggtext)
library(MASS)
library(nlme)
library(extraDistr)
library(glmnet)
library(pracma)
library(ggpubr)
library(mvtnorm)
library(knockoff)
library(bigstep)
library(parallel)
#title = expression("Moc testu t dla różnych " * beta[1] * " i " * sigma^2),
#expression("Zadanie 2b. Obciążenia " * hat(rho) * " i " * hat(gamma))
#$\mathbb{P}$
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
